headline: Interactive Brokers Group (IBKR) Stock Looks Fully Priced Despite Strong 5 Year Returns
summary: Interactive Brokers Group stock has delivered a very large 5 year gain, yet its current valuation checks lean expensive, which raises the question of how much of that success is already reflected in the price. Over 5 years, Interactive Brokers Group has returned 454.6%, which puts the recent pullback into context as a long run where early investors have already been heavily rewarded. Strong growth in client accounts, trading activity and AI enabled brokerage tools can support optimism about...

headline: Interactive Brokers (IBKR) Launches Korean Equities Trading Through Nextrade
summary: Interactive Brokers Group launched Korean equities trading for global clients via Nextrade, South Korea's first Alternative Trading System. The rollout gives clients access to extended trading hours and additional liquidity in the Korean market. This move follows the broker's role as the first major US based platform to offer direct access to Korean equities. Interactive Brokers Group, ticker NasdaqGS:IBKR, is adding another channel into South Korea at a time when interest in global equity...

headline: The AI Trade Is Looking Wobbly. These 2 Stocks Will Benefit From Increased Volatility.
summary: As the stock market alternatively displays the grace of a boxer and the oafishness of a drunkard, investors are once more confronting that which they fear most: volatility.  Buying stocks that benefit from market volatility is a useful way to hedge unrest and profit from big trading volumes.  U.S. margin debt was a record $1.4 trillion in May. If the market breaks and stocks decisively slump, trading volumes should surge.
